msgs.text:
I read the prompt and the 30-turn proposal log as an external research record. The prompt fixes the four-generator tame format: sigma,tau are distinguished, the tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2 is implicit, later generators must lie in the pro-2 core, and all profinite exponents must be represented modulo M=85667662080.The only candidate not refuted in the prior log is the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9‚Äď30; the earlier scalar, fixed ŌĄ‚ąí2, and simpler ŌÄ2 -candidates were killed by explicit finite-tame or index-two subgroup tests.

The surviving candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2‚ąąZ and ő∑=2ŌÄ on prime-to-2 tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,with ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑=‚ąí2ŌÄ ‚Č°50932630783(mod85667662080).My useful new audit point is that the two algebraic ‚Äúdirection‚ÄĚ issues in Turns 28‚Äď30 should be settled as follows.

For a norm word Na (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ, after passing to the sign double cover and a nontrivial tame-inertia character block ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1, with deck involution t, the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j=1‚ąítőĪa 1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít.This is the correct direction. Since 1‚ąítőĪa is a unit and 1‚ąít is radical, Da lies in the radical. Therefore the coefficient coming fromx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, hence a unit on every nontrivial tame-character block. So the prior conclusion that nontrivial odd tame-inertia blocks contribute no 2-primary Smith torsion is valid, but only with this corrected geometric-series formula.The second direction issue is the trivial tame-inertia block. Let A=F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1), where T is the ŌÉ-shift, and put s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), so if f=2a m with m odd then s‚Č°1(mod2a),s‚Č°0(modm).

The mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

When f is even, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. The correct elimination is therefore

Y=Q(T)X+YňČ,Q(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,not the reciprocal. With this Q, the reduced multiplier is h(T)=1+Q(T)+Q(T‚ąí1).

For a nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1,so h(ő∂)=0 would force ő∂2s+1=1. But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), hence gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Thus no nontrivial ő∂ is a zero of h, and h(1)=1. The trivial block‚Äôs cup calculation survives, but the quotient direction must be written this way.The genuinely new calculation I would add is a uniform abelianization check for the finite-tame pro-2 kernels themselves, not just their sign kernels.LetőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©,with e odd and e‚ą£2f‚ąí1. Let Ue,f be the pro-2 kernel induced by the candidate above this tame quotient, and setőĽ=e‚ąí1j=0‚ąĎe‚ąí1ŌĄňČj‚ąąZ2[őĒe,f],the idempotent projecting to the tame-inertia-invariant part. Abelianizing the orbit of the candidate relation gives, over Z2[őĒe,f],(ŌÉňČ‚ąís+őĽ)X+(őĽŌÉňČ‚ąí1)Y=0,with S=ŌÉf an additional free unramified generator.

On the (1‚ąíőĽ)-part this relation becomesŌÉňČ‚ąís X‚ąíY=0,so it contributes a free Z2 -module of rank (e‚ąí1)f and no torsion.

On the őĽ-part it reduces to the unramified relation(1+T‚ąís)X+(T‚ąí1)Y=0 over Z2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1). The Y-incidence matrix of the f-cycle gives f‚ąí1 unit Smith pivots. After those pivots are removed, the remaining cycle-sum relation is2i=0‚ąĎf‚ąí1Xi=0,which gives exactly one C2 torsion factor and reduces the free rank by one. Thus the őĽ-part contributes Z2f‚äēC2, and the marked unramified generator S contributes one more free Z2.

Therefore, uniformly for every legal finite tame quotient,Ue,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2.This is the expected local-class-field-theoretic abelianization for the maximal pro-2 Galois group of a tame degree-ef extension of Q2: free rank ef+1, with only őľ2 -torsion before adjoining i.This does not prove the candidate presentation. It does close another consistency layer and cleans up the formulas that a final proof would need. The unresolved issue is still the hard one identified in the later prior turns: the finite-tame kernels can have the correct DemuŇ°kin type, abelianization, cup product, sign kernel, and cyclotomic branch behavior while the full filtered (ŌÉ,ŌĄ)-action is not automatically determined. A final proof still needs a dyadic formation or rigidity theorem identifying the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action, includingŌÉŌÄ=ŌÉsS (1‚ąís)/f in finite unramified quotients and the dynamic tame correction ŌĄ‚ąí2ŌÄ, with the actual arithmetic Frobenius and tame-inertia actions.

ESTABLISHED: The live candidate remains unrefuted. I fixed the two recurring direction issues:Da=1‚ąítőĪa1‚ąít for nontrivial tame-character sign-cover blocks, and

Q(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís for the trivial mod-2 block. I also proved the uniform finite-tame abelianization check:Ue,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2.

GAP: This is still not a complete presentation proof. The remaining gap is the dyadic formation/rigidity step: proving that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered ŌÉ,ŌĄ-operator action on every finite-tame DemuŇ°kin kernel is the arithmetic action, not merely that the kernels have the right abelian, cup-product, and cyclotomic invariants.

NEXT: Formulate the missing rigidity theorem as an exact marked-operator comparison for(Ue,f,Ōá,S,ŌÉŌÄ,ŌĄ‚ąí2ŌÄ),then test it first in small mixed tame cases such as (e,f)=(3,2) and (5,4) at the level of U/U5, where filtered-action ambiguities first become nontrivial.[BREAKTHROUGH]
